content.full_text:
March 19, 1980


James L. Darrow
City Attorney
1275 North Main Street
El Centro, CA 92243

Dear Mr. Darrow:

     This letter confirms your letter of September 18, 1979,
that I advised you by telephone that based on the facts you
presented to our office at that time, it was not a conflict
of interest under the Political Reform Act for Mr. Garza to
vote on the Imperial Avenue overpass because of his interest
in La Donna Village.

     If you have any questions regarding this matter, please
do not hesitate to contact me.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Dwight E. Dickerson
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

DED:plh

EPPC

FEB 25 | 35 FP 80 OFFICE OF
CITY ATTORNEY

CITY HALL
1275 NORTH MAIN STREET
EL CENTRO, CA 92243
(714) 352-4011 · 352-9440

February 20, 1980


Mr. Dwight Dickerson, Legal Counsel
Conflict of Interest Division
State of California Fair Political
    Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street Bldg.
Sacramento, California  95814

Re:  Request for written confirmation of opinion

Dear Mr. Dickerson:

     As you may recall, pursuant to an inquiry made by me on behalf of Councilman
Dale Gares, a councilperson for the City of El Centro, you sent me a letter
dated September 14, 1979 confirming the advice you had given during our tele-
phone conversation of September 12, 1979.  Subsequently on September 18, 1979,
you and I had a conversation clarifying your opinion.  As you will recall, we
agreed at that time that it would send you written confirmation of our conversa-
tion clarifying your letter and that, unless you disagree with any of the con-
tents of my letter, I was free to interpret your nonresponse as an agreement
that the letter reflected your views on the matter.  You did not respond to our
letter thus satisfying us that we had correctly recited your views.

     Subsequently we communicated again on the telephone regarding another conflict
of interest question relating to La Donna Village.

     In any event, in order that my files may be documented as setting forth a complete
statement of the views of the Fair Political Practices Commission, and so that
Councilperson Gares may have his files likewise reflect the same, we hereby re-
quest that you send us a written confirmation that the foregoing understanding
regarding your letter of September 14, 1979 and our letter clarifying same dated
September 18, 1979, copies of which are enclosed herein, is correct.

     Thank you for your consideration in this regard.

Sincerely,

[signature]
James L. Darrow
City Attorney

JLD:ks
Encl.

"Where The Sun Spends The Winter"

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 607 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95314

Technical Assistance • • • Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5662           322-5660              322-5901           322-6441           322-6444

                                                                    RECEIVED
September 14, 1979                                         OFFICE OF CITY ATTORNEY
                                                           CITY OF EL CENTRO

Jim Darrow
City Attorney
1275 N. Main Street
El Centro, CA 92243                                        SEP 1 3 1979

Dear Mr. Darrow:

     The purpose of this letter is to confirm in writing the
advice I gave you during our telephone conversation of
September 12, 1979. The facts as presented to me were as
follows.

     Councilman Dale Garris has a 25% interest in a closely
held corporation known as Quad G. The corporation has a
limited partnership interest worth $12,500 in a joint venture
with Robert Childers, a developer. The Quad G/Childers
partnership owns 117 acres in the El Centro area. Fifty-five
of the 117 acres are currently being developed for light
commercial and residential use in accordance with a tentative
map approved by the city council. The remaining undeveloped
acreage has been zoned by both the city and county for low-
density residential development.

     Mr. Childers has formed other partnerships, in which
Quad G does not participate, for the purposes of developing
land parcels in the southern part of El Centro which is now
separated from the downtown area by Interstate 8. It is
contemplated that a hotel, golf course, condominium and an
athletic complex may be built in the area. Neither Councilman
Garris nor Quad G have any direct or indirect interests
in the limited partnerships formed by Mr. Childers for these
development activities. Mr. Childers, as representative of
these limited partnerships, and not the Quad C/Childers venture,
is negotiating with the city a cost sharing plan to build a
freeway overpass in order to provide access between the down-
town area and the proposed area of development.

Mr. Jim Darrow
September 14, 1979
Page Two

You have asked whether Councilman Garris is disqualified
from participating in council decisions regarding the proposed
freeway overpass. I advised you that Government Code Section
87100 requires public officials to disqualify themselves from
making or participating in decisions in which they have finan-
cial interests.

Government Code Section 87103 provides:

An official has a financial interest in a decision
within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reason-
ably foreseeable that the decision will have a
material financial effect, distinguishable from
its effect on the public generally, on:

(a) Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
more than one thousand dollars ($1,000);

(c) Any source of income, other than loans by
a commercial lending institution in the regular
course of business, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or more in value received by or
promised to the public official within twelve
months prior to the time when the decision is
made;

As a result of a 25% interest in Quad G, and Quad G's
$12,500 investment in the Quad G/Childers venture, Councilman
Garris has an investment over $1,000 in the Quad G/Childers
venture for the purposes of Government Code Sections 87100 and
87103. Thus, Councilman Garris must refrain from participating
in the freeway overpass negotiations if it is foreseeable that
the building of the overpass or the failure to build it will
materially affect Quad G or the partnership with Childers. In
addition, if it is foreseeable that the council decisions regard-
ing the overpass might affect Mr. Childers' other financial
interests to such a degree that the Quad G/Childers venture
might be financially affected, then Councilman Garris is
obligated to disqualify himself.

The Act also requires public officials to disqualify
themselves from making or participating in decisions which
foreseeably will have a material financial effect on their
sources of income. Thus, if Mr. Childers is a source of income

Mr. Jim Darrow
September 14, 1979
Page Three


to Councilman Garris, Councilman Garris should disqualify himself
from participating in the overpass negotiations. However, the
existence of a partnership relationship does not make partners
sources of income to each other.

     I hope that my advice is helpful. If I can be of further
assistance, please contact me.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Dwight Dickerson
                                        Legal Counsel
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division

DED:mfa

OFFICE OF                                                                      CITY HALL
CITY ATTORNEY                                           1275 NORTH MAIN STREET
                                                        EL CENTRO, CA 92243
                                                        (714) 352-4411 - 352-9420


                              September 13, 1979


Mr. Dwight Dickerson
Legal Counsel
Conflicts of Interest Division
State of California Fair Political
    Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street Building
Sacramento, California  95314

Re: Your Opinion Letter Dated September 14, 1979 Gares/Quad-G/Childers

Dear Mr. Dickerson:

This is to confirm our conversation of today, September 18, 1979, clarifying
your above described letter which was received by this office yesterday.
As the issues addressed in your letter may possibly be discussed at
tomorrow night's regular City Council meeting, and because the normal
time constraints which would be involved with your Office sending a
clarification letter, we have agreed that an appropriate vehicle to
provide clarification to your letter would be a confirmation letter from
me reflecting our understanding of today's telephone conversation between
us.

One question which was raised previously with your office but which was
not fully discussed in your September 14, 1979 letter was whether from
the facts presented there would appear to be a foreseeable material
financial impact to the La Donna property by reason of a decision of the
City Council to either participate in or not participate in the con-
struction of an Overpass over I-8 at Imperial Avenue. You again stated
to me that, based on the facts presented to you, it is the opinion of
the Fair Political Practices Commission that no foreseeable financial
impact exists to the La Donna venture and that Councilman Gares would not
be required to disqualify himself on that ground.

You further confirmed that the Fair Political Practices Commission has
opined that the existence of a general partner/limited partner relationship


     " Where The Sun Spends The Winter "

Mr. Dwight Dickerson
September 18, 1979
Page 2

between an applicant and a public official when the application relates
to a project which will have no foreseeable impact upon the partnership
does not constitute grounds for the public official to disqualify himself
from consideration of the application. However, you further stated that
if the public official (Councilman Cares) knows or has reason to know
facts which would lead a reasonable man to conclude that the decision
upon the application or project (in this case the I-8 Overpass) might
affect the general partners' (Mr. Childers') other financial interest
(including without limitation his other limited partnerships) to such a
degree that the developer/public official partnership (Quad-G/Childers)
might be financially affected, then the public official (Councilman Cares)
would be obligated to disqualify himself from the decision-making process
regarding the project.

Finally, you indicated that it is appropriate for Mr. Cares or his attorney
to communicate directly with you regarding this matter.

We believe that the foregoings adequately summarizes the essence of our
conversation today.

Thank you for your cooperation in this regard.

Sincerely,

[signature]
James L. Barrow
City Attorney

JLD:ks

sections.question: Whether Councilman Dale Gares is disqualified from participating in council decisions regarding a proposed freeway overpass over Interstate 8 at Imperial Avenue, given his 25% interest in Quad G corporation which has a limited partnership interest in a joint venture with developer Robert Childers who is negotiating with the city on the overpass project.
sections.conclusion: Councilman Garris must refrain from participating in the freeway overpass negotiations if it is reasonably foreseeable that the building of the overpass or the failure to build it will materially affect Quad G or the partnership with Childers. Additionally, if council decisions regarding the overpass might affect Mr. Childers' other financial interests to such a degree that the Quad G/Childers venture might be financially affected, then Councilman Garris is obligated to disqualify himself. However, the existence of a partnership relationship does not make partners sources of income to each other.
sections.question_synthetic: Is Councilman Gares required to disqualify himself from participating in council decisions regarding a proposed freeway overpass when he has a financial interest in a partnership that could be affected by the decision?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Councilman Gares must disqualify himself from the freeway overpass decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect on his Quad G/Childers partnership interest or if the decision might affect Mr. Childers' other financial interests in a way that could impact the partnership. The FPPC confirmed in follow-up correspondence that no foreseeable financial impact exists to the La Donna venture based on the facts presented.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains multiple related correspondence pieces: original advice letter dated September 14, 1979; clarification letter dated September 18, 1979; and a follow-up request for confirmation dated February 20, 1980. The primary advice letter (September 14) has clear question and conclusion sections. The document quality shows some OCR/scanning artifacts but core content is readable. There is a companion letter (September 18) that confirms and clarifies the original advice regarding the La Donna property and partnership considerations.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Councilman Dale Gares is disqualified from participating in council decisions regarding a proposed freeway overpass over Interstate 8 at Imperial Avenue, given his 25% interest in Quad G corporation which has a limited partnership interest in a joint venture with developer Robert Childers who is negotiating with the city on the overpass project.

CONCLUSION: Councilman Garris must refrain from participating in the freeway overpass negotiations if it is reasonably foreseeable that the building of the overpass or the failure to build it will materially affect Quad G or the partnership with Childers. Additionally, if council decisions regarding the overpass might affect Mr. Childers' other financial interests to such a degree that the Quad G/Childers venture might be financially affected, then Councilman Garris is obligated to disqualify himself. However, the existence of a partnership relationship does not make partners sources of income to each other.

embedding.summary: This document contains correspondence regarding conflict of interest advice given by FPPC Legal Counsel Dwight Dickerson to James L. Darrow, City Attorney for El Centro, concerning whether Councilman Dale Gares must disqualify himself from voting on a proposed freeway overpass decision. The advice letters clarify the application of California Government Code Sections 87100 and 87103 regarding financial interests and disqualification requirements.
